descript.abstract: Existing Forest Service roads with attributes representing their characteristics. Each feature represents a segment of road where the attributes are the same. Attributes apply either to the entire road or to some measured distance along the road.
descript.purpose: This feature class represents existing National Forest System (NFSR) roads necessary for the protection, administration and utilization of the National Forest. Only roads under the jurisdiction of the Forest Service are included.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: Dynamic segmentation, using the "Make Route Event Layer" process is used to segment the measured routes in each of the Forest feature classes. The resulting segmented feature classes are appended together to form an intermediate agency-wide feature class. The geometry originated as feature classes managed by individual National Forests. The feature classes managed by each National Forest are copied into a central location for processing.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Unique identifier of a route. It is a unique number within the Forest Service that is composed of a sequence number concatenated with the Oracle database instance identifier. It is a foreign key to the CN column in RTE_BASICS. It is used to join the route features to the II_Road_Core_ATM table in the ETL (Extract, Transform, and Load) process.

attrdomv.udom: Sequence Number & Oracle Instance Number string generated by Oracle

attr.attrdef: Beginning measure point of the route segment, expressed in miles.
Ending measure point of the route segment, expressed in miles.

attrdomv.udom: Ending measure point of the route segment.

attr.attrdef: The official identifier of the route.
Common name of the road
Field is populated using an SQL query SYSTEM, SURFACE_TYPE, and OPER_MAINT_LEVEL to assign the using Cartographic Feature File (CFF) to the road segment

edom.edomvd: MULTI-LANE DIVIDED HIGHWAY
HIGHWAY
ROAD NOT MAINTAINED FOR PASSENGER CARS
DIRT ROAD SUITABLE FOR PASSENGER CAR
PAVED ROAD SUITABLE FOR PASSENGER CAR
Gravel Road, Suitable for Passenger Car

attr.attrdef: Length of the segment in miles as the difference between EMP and BMP. BMP and EMP are derived from field measurements and are different than the length of the segment calculated by ArcGIS (GIS_MILES). The Seg_Length is the official length on record of the segment.

The legal right to control or regulate use of a transportation facility. Jurisdiction requires authority, but not necessarily ownership. The authority to construct or maintain a road may be derived from fee title, an easement, an agreement or some other similar method.

edom.edomvd: USDA Forest Service

attr.attrdef: A network of travel ways serving a common need or purpose, managed by an entity with the authority to finance, build, operate and maintain the routes.

edom.edomvd: A forest road other than a road which has been authorized by a legally documented right-of-way held by a State, County or other local public road authority. (36 CFR 212.1, 36 CFR 251.51, 36 CFR 261.2). Previously known as FDR - Forest Development Road.

attr.attrdef: Current physical state of being of the route segment

edom.edomvd: A route that physically exists

attr.attrdef: Operational Maintenance Level defines the level to which the road is currently being maintained. The maintenance level currently assigned to the road considering current needs, road condition, budget constraints and environmental concerns.

edom.edomvd: Assigned to roads that have been placed in storage (greater than one year) between intermittent uses. Basic custodial maintenance is performed. Road is closed to vehicular traffic
Assigned to roads open for use by high clearance vehicles.
Assigned to roads open for and maintained for travel by a prudent driver in a standard passenger car.
Assigned to roads that provide a moderate degree of user comfort and convenience at moderate travel speeds.
Assigned to roads that provide a high degree of user comfort and convenience

attr.attrdef: The maintenance level to be assigned at a future date considering future road management objectives, traffic needs, budget constraints and environmental concerns.

edom.edomvd: Assigned to roads that have been placed in storage (greater than one year) between intermittent uses. Basic custodial maintenance is performed. Road is closed to vehicular traffic
Assigned to roads open for use by high clearance vehicles.
Assigned to roads open for and maintained for travel by a prudent driver in a standard passenger car.
Assigned to roads that provide a moderate degree of user comfort and convenience at moderate travel speeds.
Assigned to roads that provide a high degree of user comfort and convenience.
Convert use of the facility to another use such as a trail.
Assigned to roads that have been or are to be decommissioned.

attr.attrdef: The grouping of roads by the character of service they provide.

edom.edomvd: An NFS road that serves smaller areas than an arterial road and that usually connects arterial roads to local roads or terminal facilities.

An NFS road that serves smaller areas than an arterial road and that usually connects arterial roads to local roads or terminal facilities. Provides service to smaller land areas than an arterial road. It usually connects forest arterial roads to local forest roads or terminal facilities.
An NFS road that connects a terminal facility with collector roads, arterial roads, or public highways and that usually serves a single purpose involving intermittent use.

attr.attrdef: The wearing course; usually designed to resist skidding, traffic abrasion and the disintegrating effects of weather.

edom.edomvd: Asphaltic Concrete
Crushed or screened graded material
Built up surface of asphalt emulsion and aggregate, not a dust palliative.
Compacted Native Material
Template has been cleared and rough shape completed but cannot be used until frozen conditions exist.
Imported or processed material (pit run, select borrow, or admixture added to the surface soil and compacted)
No imported or processed materials
Other Surface Type
Unknown manufactured hard surface
Portland Cement Concrete

attr.attrdef: The number of lanes the travel way has.

edom.edomvd: Single Lane Road
Double Lane Road
3 Lane Road
4 Lane Road

attr.attrdef: The agency or party having primary (largest share) financial responsibility for maintenance.

edom.edomvd: USDI Bureau of Indian Affairs
USDI Bureau of Land Management
USDI Bureau of Reclamation
County, Parish, Borough
Cooperator (industrial cost share)
US Army Corps of Engineers
Commercial user
US Defense Department
US Department of Energy
USDOT Federal Aviation Administration
USDA Forest Service
USDI Fish and Wildlife Service
Town, Township, Municipal Agency (City or other local civil government)
USDI National Park Service
Other Federal Agencies
Private (profit; not for profit)
State Agency
State Highway Department
State lands organizations (Department of Natural Resources)
Not known and needs to be resolved

attr.attrdef: State and County/Borough/Parish/Township in which the route segment physical resides.

attrdomv.udom: Format: SS - COUNTY where SS is the 2 digit State Code and COUNTY is name of the County.

attr.attrdef: The Congressional District where the route segment physically resides.

attrdomv.udom: Format: SS-99 where SS is 2 digit State Code and 99 is 2 digit Congressional District Number.

attr.attrdef: The Forest Service unit where the route segment physical resides or the primary Forest Service unit served by the route segment.

attrdomv.udom: Format: RRFFDD where RR is the 2-digit code for the Region, FF is the 2-digit code for the Forest, and DD is the 2-digit code for the District

attr.attrdef: The length of time that a facility is expected to provide a specified service.

edom.edomvd: Continuous or annual recurrent service
A road which is closed to vehicle traffic between periods of use. The closed period must exceed one year
Intermittent service road, closed to traffic. The road is in a condition that THERE IS LITTLE RESOURCE RISK IF maintenance IS NOT PERFORMED. (self-maintaining)
Short term use (including temporary roads)

attr.attrdef: A description of the roads significant traffic characteristics and operating conditions. The levels reflect a number of factors, such as speed, travel time, traffic interruptions, freedom to maneuver, safety, driver comfort, convenience and operating cost. Specific definition differ by facility type such as Controlled Access highways, Multilane Rural without Access Control, Two Lanes, Urban and Suburban Arterials. Level of Service is classified by flow and ADT (Average Daily Traffic) greater than or less than 400. Refer to American Association of State Highway and Transportation Officials (AASHTO) for full descriptions.

edom.edomvd: FREE FLOW > 400 ADT
REASONABLY FREE FLOW > 400 ADT
STABLE FLOW > 400 ADT
APPROACHING UNSTABLE FLOW > 400 ADT
UNSTABLE FLOW > 400 ADT

edom.edomv: F - FORCED OR BREAKDOWN FLOW > 400 ADT
edom.edomvd: FORCED OR BREAKDOWN FLOW > 400 ADT

edom.edomv: G - FREE FLOWING MIXED TRAFFIC < 400 ADT
edom.edomvd: Free flowing, mixed traffic; stable, smooth surface. Provides safe service to all traffic.

edom.edomv: H - CONGESTED WHEN HEAVY TRAFFIC < 400 ADT
edom.edomvd: Congested during heavy traffic, slower speeds and periodic dust; accommodates any legal-size load or vehicle.
Interrupted traffic flow, limited passing facilities, may not accommodate some vehicles. Low design speeds. Unstable surface under certain traffic or weather.

edom.edomv: J - SLOW FLOW OR MAY BE BLOCKED < 400 ADT
edom.edomvd: Traffic flow is slow and may be blocked by management activities. Two-way traffic is difficult, backing may be required. Rough and irregular surface. Travel with low clearance vehicles is difficult. Single purpose facility.

attr.attrdef: Federal Lands Transportation Program (FLTP) Road meets the requirements of the Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) guidance to provide access to high-use Federal recreation sites or Federal economic generators per 23 USC 203. The FLTPO classification indicates the status of FLTP designation.

edom.edomvd: Identification and inclusion in a network, of those FS roads meeting the criteria of a FLTP Designation.
A subset of miles of roads within the FLTP NETWORK-PROPOSED upon which progress will be measured and reported to the FHWA - Federal Lands Highway Division

attr.attrdef: The Managing Organization is the Forest Service unit (region/forest/district) that has the long-term responsibility for the management of the route segment.

attrdomv.udom: Format: RRFFDD where RR is the 2-digit code for the Region, FF is the 2-digit code for the Forest, and DD is the 2-digit code for the District

attr.attrdef: The Location Error is generated by the ESRI software when placing event data against a Linear Referencing System. It is used to flag errors created when matching spatial feature with corresponding Oracle database records. It indicates where there are inconsistencies between the measure point(s) of a linear event and the measure values of the spatial feature.

edom.edomvd: The route does not have a shape or the shape is empty.

edom.edomv: MEASURE EXTENT OUT OF ROUTE MEASURE RANGE
edom.edomvd: Could not find the route location's shape, the from-measure and the to-measure are outside of the route measures.
Locating was successful.
The from-measure is equal to the to-measure.
The route does not have measures or the measures are null.
The route does not exist.
Could not find the entire route location's shape, the to-measure is outside of the route measure range.
Could not find the entire route location's shape, the from-measure is outside of the route measure range.

edom.edomv: PARTIAL MARTCH FOR THE FROM MEASURE AND TO-MEASURE
edom.edomvd: Could not find the entire route location's shape, the from-measure and the to-measure are outside of the route measure range.

attr.attrdef: The length of the feature in miles, calculated in North American Albers Equal Area Conic projection (NAD83) meters and then converted to miles with the formula LengthMeters / 1609.344.

attrdomv.udom: The length of a feature in miles.

attr.attrdef: The number identifies to which organization the asset (routes, features, land units, etc.) belongs and acts as a filter that controls what asset records a user can see and edit.

attrdomv.udom: Format: RRFF where RR is the 2 digit code for the Region and FF is the 2 digit code for the Forest.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
OpenForUseTo identifies if a road segment is open to the public for motorized travel.

edom.edomvd: Open for public motorized travel for at least some portion of the year
Open for FS Administrative use for at least some portion of the year. This segment is not open for public motorized use.

attr.attrdef: The IVM Symbol is used to label road in the FS Interactive Visitor Map.

attrdomv.udom: A combination of the ID and Name field.

attr.attrdef: Descriptive name for the category of the road segment, based on the Cartographic Feature File (CFF) Code. This field often is used in map legends.

edom.edomvd: 100 - Multi-Lane Divided Highway
101 - Highway

edom.edomv: Road, Not Maintained for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: 106 - Road, Not Maintained for Passenger Car

edom.edomv: Dirt Road, Suitable for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: 515 - Dirt Road, Suitable for Passenger Car
517 - Paved Road

edom.edomv: Gravel Road, Suitable for Passenger Car
edom.edomvd: 518 - Gravel Road, Suitable for Passenger Car

attr.attrdef: A field of type UUID (Universal Unique Identifier) in which values are automatically assigned by the geodatabase when a row is created. The GlobalID field is necessary for maintaining object uniqueness across replicas. All feature classes and tables participating in one-way or two-way replication must contain the GlobalID field. This field is not editable and is automatically populated when it is added for existing data.
Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
